summary: Strong early‑70s performance with soaring Bird Song and Dark Star, captured on a decent (though bass‑light) partial soundboard recording.
review: The show is praised for high‑energy jams – Bird Song, Morning Dew, Playin', a fiery Dark Star → Brokedown Palace and an up‑tempo Don't Ease Me In – and for Jerry’s vocal brilliance. The partial soundboard source is listenable but noticeably low on bass, with occasional audience chatter that adds ambience.

ai_rating.rationale: Multiple reviewers give 4‑5 stars and highlight outstanding musical moments; recording is decent but has bass deficiencies, yielding a strong overall score.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Partial soundboard; clear vocals and instrument detail but noticeably low bass throughout. Some intermittent clapping/conversation adds ambience but no major drop‑outs.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Early‑70s set flows with big, energetic jams that peak on the major numbers; vocal performances are strong and audience interaction feels organic.

band_member_comments.Jerry: One of his best‑sung shows; guitar work praised throughout especially in Dark Star and up‑tempo Don't Ease Me In.
band_member_comments.Phil: Bass is faint on the recording, but his playing drives the Playin' jam and he’s “all over” Morning Dew and Dark Star.
band_member_comments.Keys: Ned Lagin adds a Wurlitzer electric piano and wah‑wah on Dark Star > Brokedown Palace – a rare early ’70s guest appearance with Keith Godchaux.
